methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Solid Power is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised $232M to date. The valuation universe for peer comparison is limited: only one public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers identified, including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. The sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but the narrow peer set—filtering for identical stage, adjacent development milestones, or comparable valuation tier—yields fewer than three datapoints necessary for reliable median-based valuation.

Given these constraints, peer-median triangulation is not applicable. The target lacks sufficient narrow comparables (same-stage and adjacent-stage peers combined total two; the threshold for statistical validity is three or more). Broader sector median approaches would introduce heterogeneity risk, as Climate & Energy encompasses diverse subsectors and technology maturities that are not directly comparable to Solid Power's position.

Analysis cannot produce a reliable triangulated valuation range without additional methodology (DCF, revenue multiple, or disclosed transaction data) or expansion of the comp set to adjacent sectors or development stages. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization timelines, capital intensity of scale-up, and the competitive trajectory relative to the identified private peer group. Further work should either secure comparable transaction data or apply discounted cash flow analysis if forward financials are available.
